Description: Specifies the practice name for the given practice level.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 0 then this column contains "Organization" data.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 1 then this column contains "External Service" or "Services - Level 1" or "Segment" data.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 2 then this column contains "Internal Services" or "Services - Level 2" data.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 3 then this column contains "Simplified Offering" or "Offering" data.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 4 then this column contains "Delivery Capabilities" or "Major Offering" data.
If hierarchy_depth_nbr = 5 then this column contains " Sub Offering" or "Sub Capability" data.

Description: Specifies the Name of Organization - DXC
Alias: `Practice Level 0 Name`

Description: Practice Level 1 name is also known as External Service or Services - Level 1 or Segment which is a simplified list of the services that DXC communicates to external customers and the market within the two segments of GIS and GBS.
Alias: `Practice Level 1 Name`

Description: Practice Level 2 name is also know as Internal Services or Services - Level 2 are a slightly expanded list of services that the are used internally within DXC. They provide DXC leaders and organizations an ability to apply additional focus on critical subsets of the broader External Service areas.
Some sample values:
CES = `Consulting & Engineering Services`
ITO = `IT Outsourcing`

Alias: `Practice Level 2 Name`

Description: Practice Level 3 name is also known as Simplified Offering or Offering.  Simplified Offering layer of the taxonomy identifies key areas where DXC can have discussions with customers about what it offers them to help manage and transform their business. It is at the Offering layer that DXC has the value discussion with our customers. Offerings are all about sales and account conversations about the value DXC can bring to the customers business.
Alias: `Practice Level 3 Name`

Description: Practice Level 4 name is also known Delivery Capabilities layer or Major Offering. This layer of offering of the taxonomy organizes the underlying people, process, and technology that DXC manages in order to deliver value to the customers in support of the value propositions that are described at the Offering layer.
Each Simplified Offerings has one or more Delivery Capabilities that are needed to deliver value promised to DXC customers. Delivery Capabilities enable DXC to define and contract specific solutions for our customers, and manage the delivery of those contracted commitments.
Alias: `Practice Level 4 Name`

Description: Practice Level 5 name is also known Sub Offering or Sub Capability that captures the additional level of detail for a delivery capability. A Sub Offering often defines a technical variation, but it may also define any selectable feature or functionality that requires tailoring for the customer’s needs.
What distinguishes a sub offering from a capability is the need for additional, separate solution and delivery kit collateral, especially if the component needs a separate cost model and solution pack.
Alias: `Practice Level 5 Name`
